Inside Llewyn Davis is the story of one man's quest for the acceptance of his art and who he is as a person. Llewyn Davis is a down on his luck singer songwriter in the beating heart of the burgeoning folk music scene of the East Village in New York in 1961. Having recently lost his singing partner and been forced to become a solo act Luhan eyes everyone with resentment, that he has not achieved the success he so desperately desires. he embarks on an odyssey of discovery to find himself his future and the cat he let escape. Along the way, he will travel dark roads with dangerous companions, face rejection, consider paths not taken and finally accept himself for who he is, and who he can be. About the book being co opted by libertarians, as someone who was really into objectivism when younger, it was kind of easy to see the anti government propaganda all over this book. And in my first reading, I was convinced that JL e o d, was a massive libertarian manifesto disguised as a human word hybrid lovestory. as I matured my beliefs, I understood, it's not all black and white. Nothing really is. Through all the Dudhsagar Herbert was experimenting with different political and sociological organizations pulling each of them to their limits to speculate on how they in addition to the environment shaped society over the millennia. However, one commentary that for me is clear as Frank's work is concerning centralization of power being economic power, in the hands of a monopolistic guild or decision making power in the hands of a charismatic leader, or an in cell worm boy, this is not signing up with either x nor y political orientation.
